title.en: How LLMs Work: Tokens, Embeddings & Attention
title.ta: LLM-கள் எவ்வாறு வேலை செய்கின்றன: Tokens, Embeddings & Attention

description.en: Understand the inner workings of Large Language Models — tokenization, embeddings, and the attention mechanism explained simply.
description.ta: பெரிய மொழி மாதிரிகளின் உள் செயல்பாடுகளைப் புரிந்துகொள்ளுங்கள் — tokenization, embeddings, attention mechanism எளிமையாக விளக்கப்படுகிறது.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's Brain Factory

Vaathiyaar draws a brain on the whiteboard. "Students, an LLM is like a massive prediction engine. Given 'The cat sat on the ___', it predicts 'mat' because it has seen this pattern millions of times. But HOW does it do this? Three steps: Tokenize, Embed, Attend."

### Step 1: Tokenization

Text is split into **tokens** — subword pieces, not full words.

```
'Hello, PyMasters!' → ['Hello', ',', ' Py', 'Masters', '!']
```

- GPT-4: ~100K token vocabulary
- 1 token ≈ 4 characters in English
- ₹ costs are per-token

### Step 2: Embeddings

Each token becomes a **vector** — a list of numbers representing meaning.

```
'king'  → [0.2, 0.8, 0.1, ...] (768 dimensions)
'queen' → [0.2, 0.8, 0.9, ...] (similar but different)
```

### Step 3: Attention

The model looks at ALL tokens simultaneously and decides which ones are relevant to each other.

```
'The bank by the river was steep'
'bank' attends to 'river' → means riverbank, not financial bank
```

> **Key Insight:** LLMs don't "understand" language — they predict the most likely next token based on patterns in training data. But this prediction is so good it looks like understanding.
story_variants.ta:
## வாத்தியாரின் மூளை தொழிற்சாலை

வாத்தியார் வெள்ளைப் பலகையில் ஒரு மூளையை வரைகிறார். "மாணவர்களே, LLM என்பது ஒரு பெரிய கணிப்பு இயந்திரம் போன்றது. 'The cat sat on the ___' என்று கொடுத்தால், 'mat' என்று கணிக்கிறது. ஆனால் எப்படி? மூன்று படிகள்: Tokenize, Embed, Attend."

explanation.en: tiktoken is OpenAI's tokenizer library — lets you see how text becomes tokens
Let's tokenize some text to see how LLMs see your input
Get the tokenizer for GPT-4o — each model has its own vocabulary

steps.line: text = 'Hello, PyMasters! Let us learn AI.'

explanation.en: Encode converts text into a list of token IDs (integers)
These integer IDs are what the model actually processes
Count tokens — this determines your API cost
Let's decode each token to see the subword pieces
Decode each ID back to text to see the subword piece
Display each token ID and its text representation
Now let's see how token count affects cost
API pricing is per million tokens — GPT-4o-mini is very affordable
Calculate the cost for our tokens — usually fractions of a cent

animation_sequence.caption: king - man + woman ≈ queen (vector arithmetic captures meaning)

output: Text: Hello, PyMasters! Let us learn AI.

instruction.en: Install tiktoken (pip install tiktoken) and write a function that takes a string and returns a dictionary with: token_count, tokens (list of subword strings), and estimated_cost based on $0.15 per million tokens. Test it with 'Python is the best programming language for AI development.'
instruction.ta: tiktoken நிறுவி, ஒரு சரத்தை எடுத்து token_count, tokens (subword strings பட்டியல்), மற்றும் estimated_cost ஆகியவற்றைக் கொண்ட அகராதியை திருப்பி அளிக்கும் செயல்பாட்டை எழுதுங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
import tiktoken

def analyze_tokens(text, model='gpt-4o'):
    encoder = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model)
    # Your code here: encode, decode each token, calculate cost
    pass

result = analyze_tokens('Python is the best programming language for AI development.')
print(f"Token count: {result['token_count']}")
print(f"Tokens: {result['tokens']}")
print(f"Estimated cost: ${result['estimated_cost']:.8f}")
practice_challenges.expected_output:
Token count: 9
Tokens: ['Python', ' is', ' the', ' best', ' programming', ' language', ' for', ' AI', ' development', '.']
Estimated cost: $0.00000150

en: Use encoder.encode(text) to get token IDs
Use encoder.decode([token_id]) for each token to get the text

q.en: What is a token in the context of LLMs?

en: A subword piece that the model processes

q.en: What does the attention mechanism help the model do?
q.ta: Attention mechanism மாதிரிக்கு என்ன செய்ய உதவுகிறது?

en: Understand which tokens are relevant to each other
ta: எந்த tokens ஒருவருக்கொருவர் தொடர்புடையவை என்பதைப் புரிந்துகொள்ள

q.en: Approximately how many characters equal one token in English?
q.ta: ஆங்கிலத்தில் தோராயமாக எத்தனை எழுத்துக்கள் ஒரு token-க்கு சமம்?
